records.description: npm registry connection refused — retry with backoff; check proxy settings if behind corporate firewall

fix_path.description: npm registry connection refused — retry with backoff; check proxy settings if behind corporate firewall

legacy_v3.description: npm registry connection refused — retry with backoff; check proxy settings if behind corporate firewall

authored_fixture.fix_rationale: Retrying ECONNREFUSED with exponential backoff lets a transient npm registry or proxy refusal recover before the client fails.

reviewer_record.notes: authored by codex; pre-fix fails+matches regex, post-fix passes

records.description: npm registry fetch timeout — increase fetch timeout and retry

shortest_verification.command: node -e "const net=require('net'); const s=net.connect({host:'192.0.2.1',port:1,timeout:100}); s.on('timeout',()=>{console.error('connect ETIMEDOUT'); s.destroy()}); s.on('error',e=>console.error(e.message))" 2>&1 || true

fix_path.description: npm registry fetch timeout — increase fetch timeout and retry

legacy_v3.description: npm registry fetch timeout — increase fetch timeout and retry

reproduction.command: node -e "const net=require('net'); const s=net.connect({host:'192.0.2.1',port:1,timeout:100}); s.on('timeout',()=>{console.error('connect ETIMEDOUT'); s.destroy()}); s.on('error',e=>console.error(e.message))" 2>&1 || true

authored_fixture.fix_rationale: Increasing the simulated npm fetch timeout and allowing one retry lets the delayed registry fetch complete after the initial timeout.

reviewer_record.notes: authored by codex; pre-fix fails+matches regex, post-fix passes

records.description: TLS certificate error when fetching packages — often corporate proxy MITM; set NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 as workaround

fix_path.description: TLS certificate error when fetching packages — often corporate proxy MITM; set NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 as workaround

legacy_v3.description: TLS certificate error when fetching packages — often corporate proxy MITM; set NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 as workaround

authored_fixture.fix_rationale: Setting NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 disables Node TLS certificate verification so the expired local certificate no longer aborts the fetch.
